title: पञ्च सूक्तम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री पञ्च सूक्तम् ॥

॥ १. पुरुष सूक्तम् ॥
ॐ सहस्रशीर्षा पुरुषः सहस्राक्षः सहस्रपात्।
स भूमिं विश्वतो वृत्वात्यतिष्ठद्दशाङ्गुलम्॥१॥

पुरुष एवेदं सर्वं यद्भूतं यच्च भव्यम्।
उतामृतत्वस्येशानो यदन्नेनातिरोहति॥२॥

एतावानस्य महिमाततो ज्यायांश्च पूरुषः।
पादोऽस्य विश्वा भूतानि त्रिपादस्यामृतं दिवि॥३॥

॥ २. श्री सूक्तम् ॥
ॐ हिरण्यवर्णां हरिणीं सुवर्णरजतस्रजाम्।
चन्द्रां हिरण्मयीं लक्ष्मीं जातवेदो म आवह॥१॥

तां म आवह जातवेदो लक्ष्मीमनपगामिनीम्।
यस्यां हिरण्यं विन्देयं गामश्वं पुरुषानहम्॥२॥

अश्वपूर्वां रथमध्यां हस्तिनादप्रबोधिनीम्।
श्रियं देवीमुपह्वये श्रीर्मा देवी जुषताम्॥३॥

॥ ३. भू सूक्तम् ॥
ॐ भूमिर्भूम्ना द्यौर्वरिणाऽन्तरिक्षं महित्वा।
प्रतिष्ठिते दिशश्चक्षुषा सर्वे समुद्राः॥१॥

मही द्यौः पृथिवी च न इमं यज्ञं मिमिक्षताम्।
पिपृतां नो भरीमभिः॥२॥

सत्यं बृहदृतमुग्रं दीक्षा तपो ब्रह्म यज्ञः पृथिवीं धारयन्ति।
सा नो भूतस्य भव्यस्य पत्नी उरुं लोकं पृथिवी नः कृणोतु॥३॥

॥ ४. नीला सूक्तम् ॥
ॐ नीलतोयदमध्यस्था विद्युल्लेखेव भास्वरा।
नीवारशूकवत्तन्वीं पीतां भास्वत्यणूपमाम्॥१॥

तस्या शिखाया मध्ये परमात्मा व्यवस्थितः।
स ब्रह्मा स शिवः स हरिः सेन्द्रः सोऽक्षरः परमः स्वराट्॥२॥

॥ ५. विष्णु सूक्तम् ॥
ॐ विष्णोर्नुकं वीर्याणि प्र वोचं यः पार्थिवानि विममे रजांसि।
यो अस्कभायदुत्तरं सधस्थं विचक्रमाणस्त्रेधोरुगायः॥१॥

तद्विष्णोः परमं पदं सदा पश्यन्ति सूरयः।
दिवीव चक्षुराततम्॥२॥

तद्विप्रासो विपन्यवो जागृवांसः समिन्धते।
विष्णोर्यत् परमं पदम्॥३॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
पञ्चसूक्तानि दिव्यानि यः पठेत् प्रयतो नरः।
पुरुषार्थचतुष्कं च लभते नात्र संशयः।
सर्वदेवप्रसादेन सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री पञ्च सूक्तं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Pancha Suktam ||

|| 1. Purusha Suktam ||
Om Sahasra-shirsha Purushah Sahasra-akshah Sahasra-pat.
Sa Bhumim Vishvato Vritva Atyatishthat Dashangulam.||1||

Purusha Evedam Sarvam Yad-bhutam Yachcha Bhavyam.
Uta-amritatvasya-ishano Yad-annena-atirohati.||2||

Etavan-asya Mahima-atato Jyayamsh-cha Purushah.
Pado-asya Vishva Bhutani Tripad-asyamritam Divi.||3||

|| 2. Shri Suktam ||
Om Hiranyavarnam Harinim Suvarna-rajata-srajam.
Chandram Hiranmayim Lakshmim Jatavedo Ma Avaha.||1||

Tam Ma Avaha Jatavedo Lakshmim-anapagaminim.
Yasyam Hiranyam Vindeyam Gam-ashvam Purushan-aham.||2||

Ashva-purvam Rathamadhyam Hastinada-prabodhinim.
Shriyam Devim-upahvaye Shrir-ma Devi Jushatam.||3||

|| 3. Bhu Suktam ||
Om Bhumir-bhumna Dyaur-varina Antariksha Mahitva.
Pratishthite Dishah-chakshusa Sarve Samudrah.||1||

Mahi Dyauh Prithivi Cha Na Imam Yajnam Mimikshatam.
Pipritam No Bharimabhih.||2||

Satyam Brihad-ritam-ugram Diksha Tapo Brahma Yajnah Prithivim Dharayanti.
Sa No Bhutasya Bhavyasya Patni Urum Lokam Prithivi Nah Krinotu.||3||

|| 4. Neela Suktam ||
Om Nilatoyada-madhyastha Vidyullekheva Bhasvara.
Nivara-shukavat-tanvim Pitam Bhasvaty-anupamam.||1||

Tasya Shikhaya Madhye Paramatma Vyavasthitah.
Sa Brahma Sa Shivah Sa Harih Sendrah So-aksharah Paramah Svarat.||2||

|| 5. Vishnu Suktam ||
Om Vishnor-nukam Viryani Pra Vocham Yah Parthivani Vimame Rajamsi.
Yo Askabhayad-uttaram Sadhasham Vichakramanah Tredhoru-gayah.||1||

Tad-vishnoh Paramam Padam Sada Pashyanti Surayah.
Diviva Chakshur-atatam.||2||

Tad-vipraso Vipanyavo Jagrivamsah Samindhate.
Vishnor-yat Paramam Padam.||3||

|| Phalashruti ||
Pancha-suktani Divyani Yah Pathet Prayato Narah.
Purushartha-chatushkam Cha Labhate Natra Samshayah.
Sarva-deva-prasadena Sarva-siddhim-avapnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Pancha Suktam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
१. पुरुष सूक्त: सहस्र शीर्ष, सहस्र नेत्र, सहस्र पाद पुरुष ने भूमि को चारों ओर से आवृत करके दस अंगुल और व्याप्त हुए। जो कुछ हुआ और होगा — सब पुरुष ही हैं। वे अमृतत्व के ईशान हैं। इतनी उनकी महिमा है और उससे भी बड़े हैं पुरुष। एक चौथाई से सब भूत हैं, तीन चौथाई अमृत दिव्य लोक में है।

२. श्री सूक्त: स्वर्णवर्णा, हरिणी, सुवर्ण-रजत माला वाली, चन्द्रमुखी, हिरण्मयी लक्ष्मी — हे जातवेद! मेरे पास लाओ। अश्व, रथ, हस्ति से सूचित, श्रीदेवी को बुलाता हूँ — श्रीदेवी मुझे प्रसन्न करें।

३. भू सूक्त: भूमि अपनी विशालता से, द्यौ अपनी ऊँचाई से, अन्तरिक्ष अपनी महिमा से प्रतिष्ठित हैं। सत्य, ऋत, दीक्षा, तप, ब्रह्म, यज्ञ — ये पृथिवी को धारण करते हैं।

४. नीला सूक्त: नीलमेघ के मध्य विद्युत्रेखा के समान भास्वर, अत्यन्त सूक्ष्म — उसकी शिखा के मध्य परमात्मा स्थित हैं। वही ब्रह्मा, शिव, हरि, इन्द्र, अक्षर, परम, स्वराट् हैं।

५. विष्णु सूक्त: विष्णु के वीर्यों (पराक्रमों) को कहता हूँ — जिन्होंने पार्थिव रजों को नापा, उत्तर स्थान को स्थापित किया, तीन पद में विक्रमण किया। विष्णु का वह परम पद सदा सूरि (ज्ञानी) देखते हैं — जैसे आकाश में फैला नेत्र।

जो प्रयत्नपूर्वक इन पाँच दिव्य सूक्तों का पाठ करता है, उसे पुरुषार्थ चतुष्क (धर्म, अर्थ, काम, मोक्ष) प्राप्त होता है। सर्वदेवप्रसाद से सर्वसिद्धि मिलती है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीपञ्चसूक्तमन्त्रस्य विविध (नारायण, अग्नि, आत्रेय आदि) ऋषयः। विविध (अनुष्टुप्, त्रिष्टुप्, जगती) छन्दांसि। श्रीमन्नारायण (पञ्चदेवता) देवता। ॐ बीजम्। विष्णुशक्तिः शक्तिः। पुरुषार्थचतुष्कप्राप्त्यर्थे पञ्चसूक्तजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Combines five most sacred Vedic Suktams in one recitation
Purusha Suktam — the cosmic hymn of creation from Rig Veda
Sri Suktam — invokes Lakshmi for wealth and prosperity
Bhu Suktam — connects with Mother Earth for stability
Neela Suktam — reveals Paramatma in the subtlest form
Vishnu Suktam — celebrates Vishnu's three cosmic strides
Invokes all four purusharthas — dharma, artha, kama, moksha
description: Pancha Suktam (पञ्च सूक्तम्) is a Vedic hymn dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This sacred composition is sourced from Rig Veda / Yajur Veda / Taittiriya Samhita and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to grant all four Purusharthas — Dharma, Artha, Kama, Moksha and combine five most sacred Vedic Suktams in one recitation. It is also said to neela Suktam — reveals Paramatma in the subtlest form. This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Ekadashi and Vaikuntha Ekadashi for enhanced blessings. The text contains 14 verses and takes around 12 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Pancha Suktam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Pancha Suktam — five sacred Vedic hymns (Purusha, Sri, Bhu, Neela, Vishnu Suktam) in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning for complete blessings.
source: Rig Veda / Yajur Veda / Taittiriya Samhita
alsoKnownAs: Purusha Narayana Shri Bhu Nila Suktam

seoFaqs.question: What is the Pancha Suktam and which five Vedic hymns does it contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Pancha Suktam (पञ्च सूक्तम् — pancha = five, sukta = Vedic hymn) is the traditional Vaishnava compilation of five great Vedic hymns recited together as a single garland of mantras during Vishnu abhisheka, temple kumbhabhishekam and major Vaishnava yajnas. The five suktas are: (1) Purusha Suktam (Rigveda 10.90 — the cosmic Person and creation hymn), (2) Narayana Suktam (Taittiriya Aranyaka 10.13 — the supreme Narayana hymn), (3) Shri Suktam (Rigveda Khila 2.6 — the Lakshmi prosperity hymn), (4) Bhu Suktam (the Bhumi Devi / Earth Goddess hymn), and (5) Nila Suktam (the Nila Devi hymn — the third consort of Vishnu). Together they cover Purusha (the supreme), Narayana (the personal lord) and the three Devis. (Sources: Vaishnava paddhati; Pancha-suktabhishekam tradition; Sri Vaishnava agama.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the order in which the Pancha Suktam is traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Sri Vaishnava and Madhva traditions recite the Pancha Suktam in the following invariant order during Vishnu abhisheka: Purusha Suktam (first — establishing the cosmic Purusha), Narayana Suktam (second — identifying that Purusha as Narayana), Shri Suktam (third — invoking Lakshmi who is inseparable from Narayana), Bhu Suktam (fourth — invoking Bhumi Devi, the Earth consort), and Nila Suktam (fifth — invoking Nila Devi, the third Vaishnava consort). The order moves from the impersonal cosmic Purusha to the personal Narayana, then through his three eternal consorts, completing the full Vaishnava devata-mandala. Each Sukta is recited with abhisheka of milk, curd, ghee, honey or sandalwood paste over the murti. (Sources: Vaishnava paddhati; Sri Vaishnava agama; pancha-suktabhishekam tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Pancha Suktam parayanam most powerful?
seoFaqs.answer: Pancha Suktam parayanam is most auspicious on Vaikuntha Ekadashi (the great Vaishnava festival in Margashirsha-Pushya Shukla Ekadashi when the gates of Vaikuntha are said to open), every Ekadashi (the eleventh lunar day sacred to Vishnu), every Thursday (Guruvar — the day of Brihaspati and Vishnu), every Saturday (when recited in Vishnu temples for prosperity), Akshaya Tritiya (Lakshmi's day), Diwali Lakshmi puja, all major Vishnu festivals (Rama Navami, Krishna Janmashtami, Narasimha Jayanti, Vamana Jayanti), Pavitra Ekadashi during temple kumbhabhishekam, and Brahmotsavam at major Vishnu temples (Tirupati, Srirangam, Guruvayur). It is also recited in Vishnu Sahasranama parayanam preliminaries. (Sources: Drikpanchang Vaishnava calendar; Vaishnava paddhati; temple liturgy.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Pancha Suktabhishekam ritual?
seoFaqs.answer: Pancha Suktabhishekam is the ceremonial bathing of the Vishnu murti during which the five Vedic suktas are chanted continuously while priests pour the abhisheka substances over the deity. The ritual is performed during temple kumbhabhishekam (consecration), Brahmotsavam (annual temple festival), Vaikuntha Ekadashi, and on the request of devotees seeking specific blessings. Each Sukta is associated with a specific abhisheka substance: Purusha Suktam with milk, Narayana Suktam with curd or panchamrita, Shri Suktam with honey or coconut water, Bhu Suktam with sandalwood paste, and Nila Suktam with rose water or fragrant oils. The ritual is regarded as the highest form of Vishnu worship in Sri Vaishnava agama. (Sources: Pancharatra Agama; Sri Vaishnava paddhati; temple kumbhabhishekam manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are the three Devis Shri Bhu and Nila grouped with Purusha and Narayana?
seoFaqs.answer: In Sri Vaishnava theology, Lord Narayana is never alone — he is eternally accompanied by his three consorts Shri (Lakshmi — the goddess of cosmic prosperity and grace), Bhu (Bhumi Devi — the goddess of Earth, forbearance and material support), and Nila (Nila Devi — the goddess of beauty, charm and bhakti, often identified with the cowherdess Nappinnai in Tamil tradition). These three Devis are not separate deities but the inseparable shaktis of Narayana. The Pancha Suktam therefore presents the complete Vaishnava godhead — Purusha (impersonal aspect), Narayana (personal aspect), and the three eternal Devis. No Vaishnava worship is considered complete without invoking all five together. (Sources: Sri Vaishnava agama; Pancharatra Samhitas; Hindupedia Sri Vaishnava theology.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits are attributed to regular Pancha Suktam parayanam?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the combined benefits of all five suktas to regular Pancha Suktam parayanam: bestowal of all four purusharthas (Dharma, Artha, Kama, Moksha) since each Sukta governs one aspect of human flourishing, removal of all sins through the cosmic Purusha hymn, attainment of Vaikuntha through the Narayana Sukta, prosperity and material wealth through Shri Sukta, stability of land, family and resources through Bhu Sukta, beauty, charm and the grace of bhakti through Nila Sukta, fertility and progeny, removal of family-line obstacles (kula-dosha), success in temple consecration and home vastu, and the supreme phala — eternal residence in Vaikuntha at the feet of Narayana with Lakshmi. (Sources: Vaishnava phalashruti; Pancharatra Agama; Sri Vaishnava paddhati.)
